description_long: This is a stout, wooden barrel with a classic, slightly bulging cylindrical shape, featuring three prominent metallic hoops encircling its body for structural reinforcement. The wood staves are vertically aligned, with visible dark grain lines and a warm brown coloration, giving it a rustic and robust appearance. The metallic hoops are painted or finished in a matte gray, showing some subtle wear and surface texture. The top and bottom faces are flat, with the top surface constructed from the same wood as the sides, and no visible opening or lid mechanism. There are no handles or spouts, and the surface is free from any labels, engravings, or decorative markings. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage yards, or near workshops, serving as a container or decorative object.
description: A sturdy, wooden barrel with three gray metal hoops and a rustic, brown finish. The surface is unmarked and lacks handles or a lid.
description_view_0: The barrel's front shows two small metal rivets on the upper right hoop, with the wood grain and metal bands clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side view displays the uninterrupted curvature of the barrel, with no rivets or markings, emphasizing the symmetry of the metal hoops.
description_view_2: The back view features two small rivets on the upper left hoop, mirroring the front, and highlights the even spacing of the hoops.
description_view_3: This side view shows a single rivet on the upper left hoop, with the wood and metal bands continuing seamlessly around the barrel.
